ogTitle: St George Ranges vs St James: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St George Ranges and St James.
heading: St George Ranges vs St James — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St George Ranges, Western Australia and St James, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St George Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to St James (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St George Ranges or St James safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St George Ranges has a flood risk score of 21/100, while St James scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St George Ranges scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. St James scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St George Ranges or St James?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St George Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
